run_description: Same model/architecture as 26week_30epoch_no_temporal.json, but widened the chronological sample window from 26 to 52 weeks to test whether more historical data keeps helping the way 12->26 weeks did. Result: it does NOT help -- slightly worse than the 26-week run at every epoch from 15 onward, and plateaus by epoch 20-25 instead of continuing to climb through epoch 30. Likely explanation: the earlier 12->26 week win had a verified mechanism (fixing a real cold-start sampling artifact -- 75% of '12-week-cold' customers had purchase history just outside that window). Going to 52 weeks has no equivalent story; the extra 6 months reach back into a different fashion season (late 2019/early 2020) whose patterns may not transfer well to predicting the September 2020 test week, diluting training signal with stale data rather than adding useful signal.
conclusion: More historical depth is not simply better for this domain -- there's an apparent sweet spot around 26 weeks. Recommend reverting sample_weeks to 26 as the working baseline (best result in the project: Recall@12 0.0089, MAP@12 0.0031 at epoch 30). See README.md key findings.
